title: Pipeline manifest (declarative pipeline.yaml)
description: Structure-only validation for the OPTIONAL declarative pipeline manifest (.claude/pipeline.yaml). Consumed by plugins/pipeline-core/lib/manifest.mjs with the SAME hand-rolled validator used for critic-verdict.schema.json (plugins/pipeline-core/lib/schema-lite.mjs) -- understands exactly: type, required, properties, items, enum, additionalProperties (incl. the additionalProperties-as-schema extension, used throughout below for maps whose KEYS are arbitrary names -- gate names, scanner names, model-routing roles, profile names, flag names). Semantic checks schema-lite cannot express (profiles.active must name a declared profile, profiles.*.phases[] entries must name a declared phase, phases[].condition grammar + flag existence, phases[].name uniqueness) live as CODE in manifest.mjs, not here -- do not try to shoehorn them into this file.

condition.description: Grammar always|never|<flag>|!<flag> -- checked in manifest.mjs, not here (schema-lite has no pattern keyword).

gates.description: Keys are arbitrary gate names (dev-plan, push, security, ...); every value validated against the same gate schema below.

mode.description: blocking: violations exit 2 (hard-fail) · warn: violations exit 1, never block · off: gate disabled/no-op (documented calibration, QG-06-revision rationale -- see .claude/plans/2026-07-07-ap1-pipeline-tuning.md Leitentscheidung 5).

exemptPaths.description: OPTIONAL, additive-only path-prefix exemptions for this gate (e.g. gates.dev-plan.exemptPaths, read by plugins/pipeline-core/hooks/guard-devplan.mjs) -- every entry is a plain path-prefix string, never more restrictive than the gate's built-in defaults.

scanners.description: Keys are arbitrary scanner names (gitleaks, osv-scanner, semgrep, ...).

modelRouting.description: Keys are arbitrary projected route names. Semantic constraints live in policies/model-policy.md; mutable shipped-default assignments live in routing-authority.json and are resolved by runner-mappings.json.

runnerRoutes.description: Generated provider-neutral route projection. Alias resolution is a request state, never model attestation.

profiles.description: `active` (declared in `properties` below) selects a profile name; every OTHER key is an arbitrary profile name validated against the profile schema in `additionalProperties`.

flags.description: Keys are arbitrary flag names (has_ui, ...) referenced by phases[].condition; every value must be boolean.

release.description: OPTIONAL SDLC Release/Promotion phase config (ADR-0033/0034). Absent = zero behavior change (anti-bloat). Semantic integrity (adapter references, executor/trigger/command coherence) and the central deploy-policy precedence diff live as CODE in manifest.mjs (checkReleaseIntegrity / checkDeployPrecedence), not here.

environments.description: Keys are arbitrary environment names (test, prod, ...); every value validated against the environment schema below. The canonical test/prod pair is convention, not schema law.

adapter.description: Must reference a declared release.adapters key (semantic integrity check, manifest.mjs checkReleaseIntegrity).

target.description: OPTIONAL deploy-destination id (e.g. "production-eu"). Schema-optional; SEMANTICALLY required whenever a central targets allowlist exists (precedence engine D2).

healthcheck.description: Command or workflow ref (opaque string in v0).

rollback.description: Procedure ref, or the literal "none-irreversible" -- mandatory per environment (umbrella rollback rule).

promotion.description: OPTIONAL; presence = this env carries the promote human-gate. Absent = automated env (e.g. test).

adapters.description: Keys are arbitrary adapter names (vercel-preview, vercel-prod, ...); every value validated against the adapter schema below.

trigger.description: OPTIONAL; ci executor's release-triggering ref patterns (the push guard reads these). local executor: omit refs.

command.description: OPTIONAL; local executor's deploy command ref (mutually-informative with executor:local).

credentials.description: Never inline values (SEC-08) -- the enum makes an inline secret value structurally unrepresentable.
